config.text: The FIFA World Cup 2026 Map That Tells Everything

icon.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 16 16" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="css-1i7frhi jimu-icon"><path d="M2 1a1 1 0 100 2h12a1 1 0 100-2H2zm0-1h12a2 2 0 010 4H2a2 2 0 010-4zm0 7a1 1 0 100 2h12a1 1 0 100-2H2zm0-1h12a2 2 0 010 4H2a2 2 0 010-4zm0 7a1 1 0 100 2h12a1 1 0 100-2H2zm0-1h12a2 2 0 010 4H2a2 2 0 010-4z" fill="currentColor" fill-rule="nonzero"></path></svg>

config.text: The 2026 World Cup will be held in the United States, Mexico, and Canada. This edition of the tournament, which takes place every four years, will feature 48 teams, an increase of 16 teams compared to previous editions, which comprised 32 teams. The 48 teams are allocated as follows: 16 teams from the Europe (UEFA); 10 from Africa (CAF); 9 from the Asia (AFC); 6 from the South America (CONMEBOL); 6 from North, Central America, and Caribbean (CONCACAF), including the three host nations; and 1 from Oceania. There will be three opening ceremonies: the first on June 11 in Mexico City, followed by two additional celebrations on June 12 in Toronto and Los Angeles. The final match will be played at Metlife Stadium, New Jersey.

1. Click on the ball icon for host cities to see the matches in each city and information about the city attractions.
  2. Click on county flag to see its information, national team's achievements, and more.
  3. Click the Teams and Host Cities icons on the top-right corner to switch between pages.

The 2026 World Cup will be held in the United States, Mexico, and Canada. This edition of the tournament, which takes place every four years, will feature 48 teams, an increase of 16 teams compared to previous editions, which comprised 32 teams. The 48 teams are allocated as follows: 16 teams from the Europe (UEFA); 10 from Africa (CAF); 9 from the Asia (AFC); 6 from the South America (CONMEBOL); 6 from North, Central America, and Caribbean (CONCACAF), including the three host nations; and 1 from Oceania. There will be three opening ceremonies: the first on June 11 in Mexico City, followed by two additional celebrations on June 12 in Toronto and Los Angeles.

  1. Click on the ball icon for host cities to see the matches in each city and information about the city attractions.
  2. Click on county flag to see its information, national team's achievements, and more.
  3. Click the Teams and Host Cities icons on the top-right corner to switch between pages.

where.sql: (Confederation = 'UEFA (Europe)') AND (Confederation = 'CAF (Africa)') AND (Confederation = 'CAF (Africa)') AND (Confederation = 'CONCACAF (North, Central America, and Caribbean)') AND (Confederation = 'AFC (Asia)') AND (Confederation = 'CONMEBOL (South America)') AND (Confederation = 'OFC (Oceania)')
